title: Lepidium navasii
taxonomy: Lepidium navasii is a species of plant in the family Brassicaceae, commonly known as mustard or cabbage family. It belongs to the genus Lepidium, which comprises over 160 species of plants. Lepidium navasii is a type of annual plant native to South America. This species is often found growing in dry and rocky areas. Lepidium navasii is closely related to other species within the Lepidium genus, such as Lepidium sativum, also known as garden cress.

Its medicinal uses are primarily based on its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. This plant is rich in health benefits, including its potential to lower cholesterol and blood pressure due to its high content of fiber and antioxidants.
